title: The ViT Architecture
concept: The ViT pipeline consists of patch projection, sequence construction with position embeddings, a Transformer Encoder, and a classification head.

exercises.prompt: Recap: What is the main structural component that a Vision Transformer lacks compared to traditional CNNs?
exercises.explain: Correct! Pure ViT architectures completely remove convolutional layers, replacing them with self-attention.

cards.title: The assembly line
cards.body: How do you feed a 2D image into a model designed for 1D sequences of text? You need an assembly line that chops, embeds, routes, and decodes. Let's look at the blueprint of a Vision Transformer.

cards.title: ViT Pipeline
cards.body: The ViT architecture consists of: projecting image patches into vector embeddings, prepending a learnable classification token, adding position embeddings, passing the sequence through multiple Transformer Encoder blocks, and outputting predictions from a Multi-Layer Perceptron (MLP) head.

cards.title: A mail sorting facility
cards.body: Think of the ViT architecture as a mail sorting facility: the image is chopped into packages (patches), stamped with their destination addresses (position embeddings), sent through a network of sorters who compare packages (self-attention blocks), and finally handed to a delivery manager (MLP head) who decides the final route.

cards.title: Encoder stack
cards.body: In a standard ViT-Base model, the patch sequence goes through 12 identical Transformer Encoder blocks. Each block contains multi-head self-attention and feed-forward networks, separated by layer normalization and residual connections.

cards.title: More than just self-attention
cards.body: People often focus only on the self-attention mechanism in ViT. However, the feed-forward networks (MLP layers) inside each encoder block make up a huge portion of the parameters and are crucial for learning complex feature representations.

cards.title: CNN Layering vs. ViT Blocks
cards.body: Comparing structural units of CNNs and ViTs:

left.text:
Conv -> BatchNorm -> ReLU
Downsampling via pooling
Spatial grids shrink as depth increases

right.text:
LayerNorm -> Attention -> MLP
Residual skip connections
Constant sequence length throughout

exercises.prompt: Which component in the ViT architecture is responsible for generating the final class prediction?
exercises.explain: The MLP head takes the final state of the classification token and maps it to class probabilities.

exercises.prompt: True or false: The sequence length remains constant as it flows through the ViT Encoder blocks.
exercises.explain: True. Unlike CNNs where spatial resolution decreases, ViT maintains a constant sequence length of tokens throughout its encoder.
A feed-forward network (or MLP) processes each token vector individually after the attention step.
exercises.sentence: Each Transformer Encoder block in ViT contains layer normalization, multi-head self-attention, and a ___ network.

exercises.prompt: Which of the following describes the correct order of data flow in a standard Vision Transformer?
exercises.explain: Images are first patched and projected, combined with position embeddings, processed by encoder blocks, and classified by the MLP head.

options.text: Patches -> Linear Projection -> Position Embeddings -> Encoder Blocks -> MLP Head
Encoder Blocks -> Patches -> Position Embeddings -> Linear Projection -> MLP Head
Linear Projection -> MLP Head -> Patches -> Encoder Blocks -> Position Embeddings

exercises.prompt: What is the role of residual skip connections inside the ViT Encoder blocks?

options.text: To allow gradients to flow directly through the deep stack of blocks during training without vanishing.
To reduce the sequence length by half in each block.
To discard positional information before the MLP head.
To perform spatial convolutions on the patch embeddings.

exercises.explain: Perfect! Residual connections allow deep transformers to train stably by letting gradients bypass attention layers. Next: the special token that gathers the final answer!
